disease.name: Bladder Cancer
disease.note: Most common urothelial malignancy. Urothelial (transitional cell) carcinoma >90%. Hematuria is classic presentation. Smoking is strongest risk factor.

severityAssessment.severityLabel: Non-muscle invasive (NMIBC: Ta, T1, CIS) vs Muscle-invasive (MIBC: T2+). Stage determines treatment.

subtypes.name: Non-Muscle Invasive Bladder Cancer (NMIBC)
subtypes.management: TURBT (transurethral resection of bladder tumor) - diagnostic + therapeutic. Risk stratification: low (solitary Ta LG), intermediate, high (T1, HG, CIS, >3cm, multifocal). Intravesical therapy: mitomycin C (single post-op for low risk). BCG (Bacillus Calmette-Guerin) for intermediate/high risk. Cystectomy if BCG-refractory high risk.

subtypes.name: Muscle-Invasive Bladder Cancer (MIBC)
subtypes.management: Neoadjuvant cisplatin-based chemotherapy (MVAC: methotrexate, vinblastine, doxorubicin, cisplatin or GC: gemcitabine + cisplatin) followed by radical cystectomy + extended PLND. Trimodality therapy in select patients (TURBT + RT + chemo). Adjuvant chemo if no neoadjuvant. Checkpoint inhibitors (pembrolizumab, atezolizumab) for cisplatin-ineligible or PD-L1+.

subtypes.name: Metastatic Urothelial Carcinoma
subtypes.management: First-line: cisplatin-based chemo (GC or MVAC) if eligible. Atezolizumab/pembrolizumab if cisplatin-ineligible with PD-L1+. Maintenance avelumab after chemo if no progression. Second-line: checkpoint inhibitor (pembrolizumab), enfortumab vedotin (Nectin-4 ADC), erdafitinib (FGFR altered). Palliative RT. Best supportive care.

imaging.name: CT urogram (CT IVP)
imaging.indication: upper tract evaluation, bladder mass, extravesical extension

management.protocolReference: EAU Guidelines for Non-Muscle Invasive / NCCN Guidelines for Bladder Cancer

pathways.treatment: TURBT + single dose intravesical mitomycin C (40 mg in 40 mL NS) within 24h. Surveillance: cystoscopy at 3 months, then annually.

pathways.severity: NMIBC high risk (T1, HG, CIS, multifocal)
pathways.criteria: high risk of recurrence and progression
pathways.treatment: TURBT (ensure detrusor muscle in specimen). Re-resection if T1. Induction BCG (weekly x 6) + maintenance (weekly x 3 at 3, 6, 12, 18, 24, 30, 36 months). If BCG failure: radical cystectomy or clinical trials.

pathways.criteria: muscle invasion on TURBT, no metastases
pathways.treatment: Neoadjuvant cisplatin-based chemo (GC or ddMVAC x 3-4 cycles) + radical cystectomy + extended PLND. Ileal conduit or neobladder. Trimodality in select: maximal TURBT + RT + radiosensitizing chemo.
Cisplatin-based chemo if eligible. Enfortumab vedotin. Checkpoint inhibitors. Erdafitinib if FGFR3/2 mutation. Palliative RT for bone pain/hematuria.
